rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing paper's core literature framing on global-local identity effects, not just as a generic background citation. It is explicitly invoked to support the claim that global-identity consumers favor global-brand messaging via convergent thinking, and it is also summarized in the paper's theory-building table of key prior findings. This role helps motivate the citing paper's identity-message congruence logic for environmental sustainability communication, even though the citing study does not test the cited paper's uncertainty mechanism directly.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Ng, Faraji-Rad, and Batra (2021) as prior empirical grounding in the global-local identity stream, especially on identity-congruent brand responses and associated thinking style. That foundation is used to justify the citing paper's conceptual setup about how global identity should shape responses to global brand sustainability message frames.
